question: What is the robot anatomy of the vacuum?
content:
The Roomba robot vacuum anatomy includes the top-side parts: iAdapt Localization Camera, handle, faceplate, RCON sensor, light touch sensor, dust bin and filter, and bin release button.<PIC>
Its Home Base charging station includes the RCON sensor, IR sensor window, and charging contacts.
The bottom-side parts include brush caps, dirt disposal port, dust bin, brush frame release tab, cliff sensors, floor tracking sensor, multi-surface brushes, edge-sweeping brush, charging contacts, and caster wheel.<PIC>
The controls and indicators are the light ring indicator, CLEAN / Power button, Dock / Home button, Spot Clean button, Wi-Fi indicator, troubleshooting indicator, battery indicator, full bin indicator, and Dirt Detect indicator.<PIC>
ret:
"The Roomba robot vacuum anatomy includes the top-side parts: iAdapt Localization Camera, handle, faceplate, RCON sensor, light touch sensor, dust bin and filter, and bin release button.<PIC>
Its Home Base charging station includes the RCON sensor, IR sensor window, and charging contacts.
The bottom-side parts include brush caps, dirt disposal port, dust bin, brush frame release tab, cliff sensors, floor tracking sensor, multi-surface brushes, edge-sweeping brush, charging contacts, and caster wheel.<PIC>
The controls and indicators are the light ring indicator, CLEAN / Power button, Dock / Home button, Spot Clean button, Wi-Fi indicator, troubleshooting indicator, battery indicator, full bin indicator, and Dirt Detect indicator.<PIC>",["Manual32_1", "Manual32_3", "Manual32_2"]
notes: Answered from the About your Roomba Robot Vacuum section. Three labeled figures are included for the top view, bottom view, and buttons/indicators; the Home Base parts are listed from nearby text without a separate image anchor.
